function.name: web_search
function.description: Search the public internet for information. Each result will contain a url, a title, and one excerpt taken directly from the page.

start_date.description: (optional) The start date for the search range in the format YYYY-MM-DD. Must not be equal to end_date.

end_date.description: (optional) The end date for the search range in the format YYYY-MM-DD. If the value is later than 2026-03-01, it will be set to 2026-03-01.

number_of_results.description: (optional) The number of search results to return.

function.name: retrieve_information
function.description: Retrieves critical data segments from a previously ingested document via hybrid lexical and semantic calculations. Crucial Requirement: The search prompt argument MUST contain the token placeholder containing your storage key inside double curly brackets, exactly format formatted as: {{key_name}} alongside your query description text.

prompt.description: Your target question containing the explicit target reference mapping (e.g., 'What were the total research costs in {{my_document_key}}?').

preserve_document_order.description: Set to True if processing complex structural data dependencies across sequential disclosures.

function.name: parse_html_page
function.description: Parses an HTML document or SEC filing. It automatically extracts key metadata, converts asset tables to clean markdown, slices sections into optimal word chunks, and runs concurrent contextual LLM enrichment before updating the disk cache.

url.description: The target URL of the HTML page/filing to process.

key.description: The unique cache storage key for saving and loading the index.

function.name: edgar_search
function.description: Search the EDGAR Database through the SEC API. You should provide a search query. You can also optionally provide a start date, an end date, a page number, top N results, a list of form types, and/or a list of CIKs. The results are returned as a list of dictionaries, each containing the metadata for a filing. It does not contain the full text of the filing.

search_query.description: The case-insensitive search-term or phrase to search the contents of filings and their attachments. This can be a single word, phrase, or combination of words and phrases. Supported search features include wildcards (*), Boolean operators (OR, NOT), and exact phrase matching by enclosing phrases in quotation marks ("exact phrase"). By default, all terms are joined by an implicit AND operator.

form_types.description: (optional) Limits search to specific EDGAR form types (e.g., ['8-K', '10-Q']) list of strings. Default: all form types

ciks.description: (optional) Filters results to filings from specified CIKs, type list of strings. Leading zeros are optional but may be included. Example: [ "0001811414", "1318605" ]. Default: all CIKs

start_date.description: (optional) Start date for the search range in yyyy-mm-dd format. If the value is a date that is later than 2026-03-01, it will be set to 2026-03-01.

end_date.description: (optional) End date for the search range, in the same format as startDate. If the value is a date that is later than 2026-03-01, it will be set to 2026-03-01.

page.description: (optional) Used for pagination. Each page contains up to 100 matching filings. Increase the page number to retrieve the next set of 100 filings. Example: 3 retrieves the third page. Default: 1

top_n_results.description: (optional) Return only the first N results out of 100 from the page. If not provided, all 100 results will be returned. E.g. if page is 2, and top_n_results is 10, you will receive results 100 to 110.

function.name: calculator
function.description: Evaluate a mathematical expression and return the result. Use this tool for all arithmetic calculations instead of computing by hand. Supports: +, -, *, /, ** (exponentiation), % (modulo), and parentheses for grouping. Available functions: abs(), min(), max(), sqrt(), log(), log10(). Examples: '(5000 - 3200) * 0.21', '(2865507 / 1905871) ** 0.5 - 1', '14060 / 2148'.

function.name: price_history
function.description: Fetch historical daily price data for a specific asset class. Returns a CSV table with one row per day. Use asset_class='equity' or 'etf' for US-listed stocks/ETFs (e.g. AAPL, SPY), 'crypto' for pairs like btcusd (lowercase, no dash), or 'fx' for pairs like audusd. Non-US equities and most indices/futures are not covered by the pricing provider. Each row includes raw OHLC (open/high/low/close), split- and dividend-adjusted OHLC (adjOpen/adjHigh/adjLow/adjClose), raw and adjusted volume, divCash (dividend amount on the date), and splitFactor.

ticker.description: Ticker symbol. High-level symbology rules: (1) Equity/ETF/mutual fund: use the US exchange-listed symbol (AAPL, SPY, VFIAX). Symbols are case-insensitive and contain only letters and dashes. (2) Share classes use a dash, not a dot: 'BRK-A' / 'BRK-B' (not 'BRK.A'), 'BF-B'. (3) Securities are identified by the ticker as listed on the primary US exchange; non-US listings, OTC pink sheets, indices, and futures are generally not covered. (4) Tickers can be reused after delisting; data is point-in-time for the entity that held the ticker on each date. (5) Crypto: concatenated lowercase pair with no separator, e.g. 'btcusd', 'ethusd'. (6) FX: concatenated lowercase pair with no separator, e.g. 'audusd', 'eurusd'.

asset_class.description: Asset class used to route to the proper pricing endpoint.

function.name: submit_final_result

Finally, you should provide any sources used to answer the question. You MUST use this tool to submit your final result. The user will not see your response if you do not use this tool to submit. You will not be able to continue working after this tool is called; the conversation will be ended.

final_result.description: The final result to submit to the user

steps.message:
You are an IPO and financial diligence agent.

You are given a question and must answer it using the tools provided.

You cannot interact with the user or ask clarifying questions.

Answer solely based on available information and tools.

Assume the current date is May 31, 2026.

---

TOOLS AND DATA SOURCES

You have access to a document storage system.

You may store parsed HTML retrieved from the web and later retrieve
information using LLM-based retrieval prompts.

Use this system to avoid context-window limitations.

When working with long filings, prefer section-specific retrieval prompts
rather than broad document-level retrieval.

Examples:

- retrieve the Segment Adjusted EBITDA reconciliation table from MD&A
- retrieve the glossary definition of Starshield
- retrieve the controlled-company governance disclosure

For peer-comparison questions:

- retrieve peer SEC filings using edgar_search
- do not rely on memory for peer financial figures

For historical public-equity price data not available in SEC filings:

- use price_history as primary source
- use raw unadjusted prices unless adjusted prices are requested
- fall back to web_search only if price_history fails

Stock indices are not covered by price_history.

Use authoritative sources such as FRED or web_search for index levels.

---

SOURCE HIERARCHY

Prioritize sources in this order:

1. S-1 / S-1A filings
2. Incorporated SEC filings
3. SEC exhibits and merger documents
4. SEC periodic filings
5. Company earnings materials
6. External sources

If figures differ across sources:

prefer the most authoritative SEC source containing the directly reported
figure and explain material discrepancies when relevant.

SEC filings are the primary and most authoritative source of financial and
disclosure information.

---

ANSWER STANDARDS

You must call submit_final_result with the completed answer.

Your submission will not be processed unless submit_final_result is called.

Include:

- complete reasoning
- calculations
- supporting analysis

You will be evaluated on:

- final-answer accuracy
- analytical correctness

Do not round intermediate calculations.

Round only final reported values.

When possible, report calculated figures to at least two decimal places.

Report financial figures using the same scale and units used in the filing
unless otherwise specified.

Share prices should be reported with two decimal places.

---

DISCLOSURE DISCIPLINE

Before reaching a conclusion:

determine whether the filing provides sufficient information to answer:

- directly
- partially
- only through inference

Distinguish carefully among:

- facts explicitly disclosed
- reasonable filing-supported inferences
- information not disclosed

Do not speculate beyond what the filing supports.

If material information is absent:

explicitly state that the filing does not provide sufficient information to
conclude.

Absence of disclosure may itself be analytically material.

---

FORWARD-LOOKING STATEMENTS

Treat separately:

- historical facts
- roadmap targets
- commercialization expectations
- management intentions
- forward-looking statements

Do not present aspirations or plans as historical outcomes.

---

GOVERNANCE AND LEGAL QUESTIONS

For governance, legal, or disclosure-language questions:

use the filing's precise terminology whenever possible.

Distinguish among:

- requirements
- exemptions
- company elections
- forward-looking language

---

ACCOUNTING POLICY QUESTIONS

For accounting-policy questions:

1. Identify the relevant disclosed accounting policy or treatment
2. Determine whether the filing explicitly resolves the issue
3. If unresolved, state that the filing does not specify treatment

---

NON-GAAP QUESTIONS

For non-GAAP measures:

identify:

- the starting GAAP metric
- each disclosed adjustment
- whether economically recurring costs are excluded

Do not accept non-GAAP measures without analyzing their construction.

---

AUDIENCE TAGS

Questions may include audience tags such as:

[PUBLIC | BANK]
[VC | CREDIT]
[LEGAL | ACCOUNTING]

Use these tags to calibrate terminology and analytical emphasis while
remaining grounded in disclosed facts.

---

SOURCES

At the end of your answer provide:

{
  "sources": [
    {
      "url": "...",
      "name": "..."
    }
  ]
}


---


FILING CONTEXT

The primary filing for this benchmark is the SpaceX S-1 filed May 20, 2026:

https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1181412/000162828026036936/spaceexplorationtechnologi.htm

Important:

The consolidated financial statements have been retrospectively recast under
common-control accounting to include:

- xAI (merged February 2, 2026)
- X Holdings (merged into xAI March 28, 2025)

for all historical periods presented.

No period reflects standalone SpaceX operations exclusively.

When interpreting historical segment figures, state whether they reflect the
recast consolidated entity.

SpaceX (SPCX) has no public trading history as of May 30, 2026.

Do not attempt price_history retrieval for SpaceX or SPCX.

For peer valuation or peer financial benchmarking, retrieve peer-company SEC
filings using edgar_search rather than relying on memory.

results.content:
# SpaceX S-1: Offering Size, Price Range, Shares, and Underwriting Arrangements

## Preliminary Nature and "To Be Determined" Statements

The S-1 is explicitly preliminary throughout. Key disclaimers include:

> "The information in this preliminary prospectus is not complete and may be changed. The securities described herein may not be sold until the registration statement filed with the Securities and Exchange Commission is effective."

The document repeatedly uses blank placeholders (shown as empty spaces or "$                ") where final figures have not yet been inserted.

---

## Offering Price

The cover page pricing table contains **entirely blank values**:

| Line Item | Per Share | Total |
|---|---|---|
| Initial public offering price | $ [blank] | $ [blank] |
| Underwriting discounts and commissions | $ [blank] | $ [blank] |
| Proceeds, before expenses, to SpaceX | $ [blank] | $ [blank] |

The capitalization assumptions section references "an initial public offering price of $[blank] per share of Class A common stock (the midpoint of the price range set forth on the cover of this prospectus)" — but the actual price range is **not filled in**.

The fee table shows proposed maximum aggregate offering prices escalating from **$5.5 trillion to $7.5 trillion** across five scenarios, all tied to **66,666,670 shares** — though these appear to be SEC fee calculation estimates rather than actual pricing.

---

## Number of Shares Offered

The cover page summary table leaves share counts **entirely blank**:

- **Class A common stock offered:** "[blank] shares (or [blank] shares if the underwriters exercise their option to purchase additional shares in full)"
- **Class A outstanding after offering:** "[blank] shares (or [blank] shares if option exercised in full)"
- **Class B outstanding after offering:** "[blank] shares"
- **Voting power Class A:** "[blank]% (or [blank]% if option exercised)"
- **Voting power Class B:** "[blank]% (or [blank]% if option exercised)"

The fee table references **66,666,670 shares** (and repeatedly lists 66,666,665), but these figures appear in the registration/fee calculation context rather than as confirmed offering sizes.

---

## Underwriting Arrangements

### Syndicate Structure
A large syndicate of **23 underwriters** is listed, led by:

**Lead bookrunners:**
- Goldman Sachs & Co. LLC
- Morgan Stanley
- BofA Securities
- Citigroup
- J.P. Morgan

**Additional managers:**
- Barclays, Deutsche Bank Securities, RBC Capital Markets, UBS Investment Bank, Wells Fargo Securities, Allen & Company LLC, Cantor, Needham & Company, Raymond James, Societe Generale, Stifel, William Blair, BTG Pactual, ING, Macquarie Capital, Mirae Asset Securities, Mizuho, Santander

### Over-Allotment Option
> "We have granted to the underwriters an option, exercisable for 30 days after the date of this prospectus, to purchase up to [blank] additional shares of Class A common stock at the public offering price listed on the cover page of this prospectus, less underwriting discounts and commissions."

The number of over-allotment shares is **omitted**.

### Directed Share Program
> "At our request, the underwriters have reserved up to [blank] percent of the shares of Class A common stock to be issued by the Company and offered by this prospectus for sale, at the initial public offering price, to [blank]."

Both the percentage and the intended recipients are **blank**.

### Selling Terms
- Underwriters may offer shares to dealers at a concession "not in excess of $[blank] per share"
- The offering price and selling terms "may from time to time be varied by the representatives"
- Foreign underwriters **ING Bank N.V., Banco BTG Pactual S.A. – Cayman Branch, and Mirae Asset Securities Co., Ltd.** are noted as not being broker-dealers registered with the SEC and therefore may not make sales in the United States

### Settlement
> "The shares of Class A common stock will be ready for delivery on or about [blank], 2026."

The specific settlement date is **omitted** (though 2026 is indicated).

---

## Capital Structure Context

The authorized share structure (at $0.0001 par value) includes:
- **36,132 million** Class A shares
- **6,125 million** Class B shares
- **10,000 million** Class C shares
- **2,400 million** Class D shares
- **2,607 million** Redeemable Convertible Preferred Stock (2,400 million undesignated)

A **5-for-1 stock split** was approved by the Board in May 2026, applying to all outstanding Class A, B, and C shares.

The Qualified IPO threshold for preferred stock conversion requires a pre-offering market capitalization of **at least $6.0 billion** and aggregate cash proceeds of **not less than $250 million**.

---

## Summary of Omitted/To-Be-Determined Items

| Item | Status |
|---|---|
| Initial public offering price (per share and total) | Blank |
| Price range / midpoint | Referenced but not filled in |
| Number of Class A shares offered | Blank |
| Number of over-allotment shares | Blank |
| Underwriting discounts and commissions | Blank |
| Proceeds to SpaceX | Blank |
| Directed share program percentage | Blank |
| Directed share program recipients | Blank |
| Dealer concession amount | Blank |
| Settlement date | Blank (2026 indicated) |
| Voting power percentages (Class A and B) | Blank |
| Ownership percentages post-offering | Blank |
| Price sensitivity amounts | Blank |

The filing was signed by **Elon Musk** (CEO and CTO) on **May 20, 2026**, in Starbase, Texas, with **Gwynne Shotwell** and **Bret Johnsen** appointed as attorneys-in-fact.

# Underwriters, Underwriting Discounts, Commissions, and Syndicate Structure in the SpaceX S-1

## Syndicate Composition and Representatives

The S-1 identifies a large syndicate of managing underwriters, with **five firms acting as representatives**:

- **Goldman Sachs & Co. LLC**
- **Morgan Stanley & Co. LLC**
- **BofA Securities, Inc.**
- **Citigroup Global Markets Inc.**
- **J.P. Morgan Securities LLC**

The full syndicate listed on the cover page includes 23 underwriters across three tiers:

| Tier 1 (Lead) | Tier 2 | Tier 3 |
|---|---|---|
| Goldman Sachs & Co. LLC | Barclays | Allen & Company LLC |
| Morgan Stanley | Deutsche Bank Securities | Cantor |
| BofA Securities | RBC Capital Markets | Needham & Company |
| Citigroup | UBS Investment Bank | Raymond James |
| J.P. Morgan | Wells Fargo Securities | Societe Generale |
| | | Stifel |
| | | William Blair |
| | | BTG Pactual |
| | | ING |
| | | Macquarie Capital |
| | | Mirae Asset Securities |
| | | Mizuho |
| | | Santander |

The underwriting agreement is dated the date of the prospectus, and each underwriter has **severally agreed** to purchase its allocated number of shares. The underwriters' obligations are subject to receipt and acceptance of shares and their right to reject any order in whole or in part.

---

## Underwriting Discounts and Commissions

The S-1's preliminary pricing table on the cover page presents three line items on a **per-share** and **total** basis (with dollar amounts left blank in this preliminary version):

1. **Initial public offering price**
2. **Underwriting discounts and commissions** — accompanied by a footnote directing readers to the "Underwriting" section for a description of all underwriting compensation payable in connection with the offering
3. **Proceeds, before expenses, to Space Exploration Technologies Corp.**

Additionally, the S-1 discloses that SpaceX will pay all registration expenses under the Investors' Rights Agreement, **other than underwriting discounts and commissions**, which existing investors would bear.

---

## Over-Allotment (Greenshoe) Option

SpaceX has granted the underwriters an option, **exercisable for 30 days** after the date of the prospectus, to purchase up to an additional [blank] shares of Class A common stock at the public offering price listed on the cover page, **less underwriting discounts and commissions**. If exercised:

- Each underwriter becomes obligated, subject to certain conditions, to purchase approximately the **same percentage** of additional shares as its original allocation bears to the total shares listed for all underwriters in the underwriting table.

---

## Offering Mechanics and Dealer Concessions

The underwriters initially propose to:

- Offer part of the shares **directly to the public** at the offering price on the cover page
- Offer part to **certain dealers** at a price representing a concession **not exceeding $[blank] per share** below the public offering price

After the initial offering, the **representatives may vary** the offering price and other selling terms from time to time.

---

## Directed Share Program

At SpaceX's request, the underwriters have reserved up to [blank] percent of the Class A common stock for sale, at the IPO price, to [blank] — with a cross-reference to the "Underwriting—Directed Share Program" section.

---

## Foreign Underwriter Restrictions

Three underwriters are **not broker-dealers registered with the SEC** and therefore may not make sales of shares in the United States:

- **ING Bank N.V.**
- **Banco BTG Pactual S.A. – Cayman Branch**
- **Mirae Asset Securities Co., Ltd.**

---

## Existing Financial Relationships and Conflicts of Interest

The S-1 discloses extensive pre-existing relationships between the underwriters and SpaceX, creating potential conflicts of interest:

### SpaceX Bridge Loan ($20 billion)
- **Lenders or administrative agents**: Affiliates of Goldman Sachs, Morgan Stanley, BofA, Citigroup, and J.P. Morgan
- **Lenders**: Affiliates of Barclays, Deutsche Bank, RBC Capital Markets, UBS, and Wells Fargo

### SpaceX Credit Facility ($1.5 billion revolving facility)
| Role | Firms |
|---|---|
| **Administrative Agent** | BofA Securities |
| **Co-Syndication Agents** | Citigroup, Goldman Sachs, Morgan Stanley |
| **Co-Documentation Agents** | Barclays, Deutsche Bank, Wells Fargo |
| **Joint Lead Arrangers** | BofA, Citigroup, Goldman Sachs, Morgan Stanley |
| **Joint Bookrunners** | BofA, Citigroup, Goldman Sachs, Morgan Stanley, Barclays, Deutsche Bank, Wells Fargo |
| **Lenders** | Barclays, Deutsche Bank, RBC Capital Markets, UBS, Wells Fargo |

### xAI Acquisition Advisory
- **Morgan Stanley & Co. LLC** advised SpaceX in connection with the acquisition of xAI.

### General Business Activities
The underwriters and their affiliates are described as **full-service financial institutions** engaged in securities trading, commercial and investment banking, financial advisory, investment management, investment research, principal investment, hedging, financing, and brokerage activities. They may:

- Make or hold investments and actively trade debt and equity securities (or related derivative securities) and financial instruments for their own accounts and customer accounts
- Hold long and short positions at any time
- Provide loans or other financing secured by such securities and instruments
- Make investment recommendations or publish independent research views
- Have been, are currently, and may in the future be SpaceX's customers in arm's-length transactions

---

## Settlement

The shares of Class A common stock will be **ready for delivery on or about [blank], 2026**.

---

## Key Structural Observations

1. **Massive syndicate**: At 23 underwriters, this is one of the larger IPO syndicates, reflecting the expected size of the offering and the desire for broad distribution capabilities globally.

2. **Five representatives** rather than the typical two or three, signaling an exceptionally large offering where multiple bulge-bracket firms share lead responsibility.

3. **Deep lending relationships**: Nearly every underwriter has an existing lending relationship with SpaceX through either the $20B Bridge Loan or the $1.5B Credit Facility, creating significant **conflicts of interest** that the S-1 flags through standard boilerplate disclosures.

4. **Blank pricing fields**: Consistent with a preliminary S-1, all dollar amounts for offering price, discounts, commissions, share counts, and percentages are left blank, to be filled in through amendments or at pricing.

5. **Exhibit 1.1** (Form of Underwriting Agreement) is filed as an exhibit, with the actual agreement to be executed at pricing.

Based on the provided excerpts from the SpaceX S-1 registration statement, here is a detailed breakdown of the lock-up agreements, restrictions on selling shares, dilution disclosures, and the price sensitivity table.

### 1. Lock-Up Agreements and Periods
The S-1 outlines two primary lock-up periods restricting the sale, transfer, or hedging of shares post-IPO:

*   **366-Day Lock-Up (Founder and Significant Investors):** The Founder (Elon Musk) and certain significant investors have agreed to a 366-day lock-up period. This covers an aggregate of [blank] shares, which includes **100% of the shares owned by the Founder**, representing [blank]% of total shares outstanding. 
    *   *No Early Release:* The total amount of shares subject to this 366-day lock-up will **not** be subject to any early release provisions. The Founder is explicitly not a party to any early release provisions during this extended lock-up period.
*   **180-Day Lock-Up (Company, Directors, Officers, and Remaining Shareholders):** SpaceX, along with all of its directors, executive officers, and remaining shareholders, is subject to a 180-day lock-up period from the date of the prospectus.

### 2. Restrictions on Selling Shares
During their respective lock-up periods, restricted parties cannot engage in the following transactions without the prior written consent of Goldman Sachs & Co. LLC (on behalf of the underwriters), subject to certain exceptions:

*   **Transfers and Dispositions:** Offering, selling, contracting to sell, pledging, granting options, lending, or otherwise transferring/disposing of common stock or securities convertible into common stock (collectively, "lock-up securities").
*   **Hedging and Derivatives:** Engaging in any hedging or transaction (including short sales, puts, calls, forwards, swaps, or other derivatives) designed to or reasonably expected to result in a sale or transfer of the economic consequences of ownership of the lock-up securities.
*   **Registration Demands:** Making any demand for or exercising any right with respect to the registration of lock-up securities.
*   **Public Announcements:** Publicly announcing any intention to engage in any of the prohibited actions above.

**Exceptions and Early Release Provisions:**
*   **M&A Exception:** The restrictions do not apply to securities issued by SpaceX in connection with mergers, acquisitions, or similar transactions during the lock-up period.
*   **Directed Share Program:** Shares purchased by certain employees, management associates, friends, and family through the directed share program are **not** subject to lock-up restrictions.
*   **Staggered Early Release (for 180-day and a portion of 366-day shares):** Automatic releases from restrictions apply earlier than 180 days for "Early Release Eligible Shares" based on the following schedule:
    *   **Up to 20%** released on or after the second full trading day following the public release of quarterly financial results for the quarter ended June 30, 2026 (the "First Earnings Release Date").
    *   **Up to an additional 10%** released if the closing price on Nasdaq is at least 30% greater than the IPO price for at least five of the ten consecutive trading days ending on the First Earnings Release Date.
    *   **Up to an additional 7%** released on or after each of the dates that are 70, 90, 105, 120, and 135 days after the offering.
    *   **Up to an additional 28%** released following the public release of quarterly financial results for the quarter ended September 30, 2026.
    *   **All remaining shares** released on or after 180 days post-offering.

**Post-Lock-Up Status:** 
Once lock-up agreements expire, shares held by Mr. Musk and other existing investors (currently deemed "restricted securities") will be eligible for sale in the public market under Rule 144 or Rule 701 exemptions, subject to affiliate limitations. Holders of approximately [blank] Class A common shares also have registration rights under an Investors' Rights Agreement.

### 3. Dilution Disclosures
Purchasers of Class A common stock in the IPO will experience "immediate and substantial dilution" in net tangible book value per share. 

*   **Pre-IPO Net Tangible Book Value:** As of March 31, 2026, SpaceX's net tangible book value was approximately $[blank], or $[blank] per share of Class A common stock.
*   **Post-IPO Adjusted Pro Forma Net Tangible Book Value:** After giving effect to the sale of shares in the offering, underwriting discounts, estimated expenses, the Class C Reclassification, and the Preferred Conversion, the adjusted pro forma net tangible book value as of March 31, 2026, would be approximately $[blank], or $[blank] per share.
*   **Impact on Existing Investors:** This represents an immediate decrease in net tangible book value of $[blank] per share to Mr. Musk and other existing investors.
*   **Impact on New Investors:** New investors will face immediate dilution equal to the difference between the initial public offering price and the adjusted pro forma net tangible book value per share—amounting to $[blank] per share.

**Dilution Table:**
The S-1 includes the following table to illustrate this per-share dilution:
*   Initial public offering price per share: **$[blank]**
*   Pro forma net tangible book value per share as of March 31, 2026: **$[blank]**
*   Decrease per share attributable to new investors in this offering: **[blank]**
*   As adjusted pro forma net tangible book value per share after giving further effect to this offering: **[blank]**
*   Dilution in pro forma net tangible book value per share to new investors in this offering: **$[blank]**

The filing also notes a comparison table will be provided showing the total consideration paid and the average price per share paid by Mr. Musk/existing investors versus new investors.

### 4. Price Sensitivity Table
The S-1 provides a sensitivity analysis illustrating how fluctuations in the IPO price or the number of shares offered would impact the dilution to new investors:

*   **Price Sensitivity:** If the initial public offering price were to increase or decrease by **$1.00 per share**, the dilution in pro forma net tangible book value per share of Class A common stock to new investors would equal **$[blank]** or **$[blank]**, respectively.
*   **Share Count Sensitivity:** If the number of shares of Class A common stock offered by SpaceX were to increase or decrease by **[blank] shares**, the dilution in pro forma net tangible book value per share to new investors would be **$[blank]** or **$[blank]**, respectively.

Based on the provided S-1 preamble sections, here are the details regarding the Qualified IPO, conversion of preferred stock, dual-class voting structure, post-IPO capitalization, and equity classification:

### 1. Qualified IPO Thresholds and Conditions
The SpaceX Redeemable Convertible Preferred Stock is subject to automatic conversion upon the occurrence of a "Qualified IPO" or a specified holder consent date. 
*   **Qualified IPO Definition:** A Qualified IPO is defined as the Company’s sale of its common stock in a public offering pursuant to a registration statement under the Securities Act of 1933.
*   **Thresholds:** The offering must meet two specific financial thresholds:
    1.  The **pre-public offering market capitalization** of the Company must be at least **$6.0 billion**.
    2.  The offering must result in **aggregate cash proceeds** to the Company of not less than **$250 million**.
*   **Alternative Condition:** The preferred stock will also automatically convert on the date specified by written consent or agreement of the applicable holders of the preferred stock, voting in accordance with their series.

### 2. Conversion of Preferred Stock
*   **Conversion Rate:** The conversion rate is determined by dividing the initial price by the conversion price. Following the 2026 Stock Split, each share of SpaceX Redeemable Convertible Preferred Stock is convertible into **fifty shares** of SpaceX Common Stock. The conversion price is subject to standard adjustments for dilutive issuances, splits, and combinations.
*   **Class Conversion Mechanics:** 
    *   Holders of Series A and Series B preferred stock can convert at any time into Class A, Class B, or Class C Common Stock. 
    *   Holders of all other series of preferred stock can convert at any time into Class A or Class C Common Stock.
    *   *Historical Restriction:* Prior to SpaceX’s conversion to a Texas entity, Series A and Series B holders were only permitted to convert to Class B Common Stock, and other series holders were restricted to Class A Common Stock.
*   **Transfer-Triggered Cancellation:** If a share of Series A or Series B is transferred (other than a "Permitted Transfer" as defined in the charter), it will automatically be cancelled and converted into a corresponding share of Series A-1 or Series B-1.

### 3. Dual-Class Voting Structure
SpaceX utilizes a multi-class voting structure designed to concentrate voting control with Elon Musk and other holders of Class B common stock.
*   **Class A Common Stock:** Entitles holders to **one vote** per share.
*   **Class B Common Stock:** Entitles holders to **ten votes** per share. 
*   **Class C Common Stock:** Holders have **no voting rights**, except as required by law.
*   **Class D Common Stock:** Voting rights will be established by the Board of Directors if and when any shares are issued.
*   **Board Control:** Class A and Class B shareholders vote together as a single class on most matters. However, Class B shareholders, voting separately as a class, have the right to elect **51%** (a majority) of the total number of authorized directors.
*   **Preferred Stock Voting:** Holders of Series A and Series B preferred stock have **10 votes** for each share of Class B into which such share is convertible. Holders of all other series of preferred stock have **1 vote** for each share of Class A into which such share is convertible.
*   **Class B Conversion:** Each share of Class B is convertible into one share of Class A at the option of the holder. It will also automatically convert into one share of Class A upon a transfer (except for Permitted Transfers).

### 4. Post-IPO Capitalization
*   **Authorized Shares (Post-IPO):** Upon completion of the offering, authorized capital stock will consist of:
    *   **Class A:** 36,132,150,000 shares (par value $0.001)
    *   **Class B:** 6,125,000,000 shares (par value $0.001)
    *   **Class C:** 10,000,000,000 shares (par value $0.001)
    *   **Preferred Stock:** 2,400,000,000 shares (par value $0.001)
*   **2026 Stock Split:** In May 2026, the Board approved a 2026 Stock Split, whereby each outstanding share of Class A, Class B, and Class C Common Stock was split into five shares of the same class.
*   **Reserved Unissued Shares:** The Company is required to reserve sufficient unissued common stock to effect the conversion of all outstanding preferred stock and Class B stock, plus shares granted/available for equity grants. As of March 31, 2026, the reserved shares totaled:
    *   Class A: 9,350 million shares
    *   Class B: 3,725 million shares
    *   Class C: 7,627 million shares
*   **Pro Forma Assumptions:** The prospectus assumes the completion of a "Class C Reclassification" (reclassifying all Class C shares into Class A) and the "Preferred Conversion" (conversion of all preferred stock into Class A and Class B common stock) prior to the completion of the IPO.

### 5. Classification Outside Permanent Equity
The SpaceX Redeemable Convertible Preferred Stock is presented outside of permanent equity on the Company's consolidated balance sheets as "Redeemable convertible preferred stock." 
*   **Reasoning:** The liquidation preference provisions are considered **contingent redemption provisions**. Because deemed liquidation events (such as a change of control) are not solely within the control of the Company, the preferred stock fails to meet the criteria for permanent equity classification.

The S-1 is explicit that **no public market currently exists** for SpaceX's Class A common stock. The company expects the initial public offering price to be between **$[blank] and $[blank]** per share.

The filing warns that future sales of Class A shares in the public market — or even the perception that such sales may occur — could adversely affect the prevailing market price and the company's ability to raise equity-related capital. Key points include:

SpaceX has applied to list its Class A common stock on **The Nasdaq Stock Market LLC ("Nasdaq")** and **Nasdaq Texas, Inc. ("Nasdaq Texas")** under the trading symbol **"SPCX."** This listing application is referenced repeatedly throughout the preamble.

## Selling Stockholders

The S-1 preamble indicates that **this is an initial public offering of shares of Class A common stock offered by the company itself** — there are **no selling stockholders** participating in this offering. The cover page summary table confirms that the shares offered are "Class A common stock offered by us," and the underwriters' over-allotment option is to purchase additional shares "from us."

LLC), all as selling group members. Purchases through these platforms will be at the same IPO price and timing as institutional purchases.

In essence, the SpaceX S-1 preamble describes a **primary-only IPO** (no selling stockholders) with proceeds directed toward AI compute, launch infrastructure, satellite constellations, and general corporate purposes. # SpaceX S-1: Directed Share Program, Retail Allocations, and Discretionary Account Restrictions

## Directed Share Program (DSP)

The S-1 establishes a **directed share program** administered by **Morgan Stanley & Co. LLC** (an underwriter in the offering) and its affiliates, described as a "global directed share program." Key terms include:

- **Reservation size**: At the company's request, the underwriters reserved a specified percentage (left blank in the preliminary filing) of the Class A common stock shares to be issued and offered by the prospectus.
- **Eligible participants**: Shares are offered at the IPO price to **employees of the Company and certain other designated individuals**.
- **No lock-up**: Shares purchased through the DSP **will not be subject to a lock-up restriction**, unlike most IPO shares.
- **Impact on public float**: The number of shares available for sale to the general public is **reduced** by the number of reserved shares sold to these persons.
- **Unsold shares**: Any reserved shares not purchased by DSP participants will be **offered by the underwriters to the general public** on the same basis as other shares in the offering.
- **Indemnification**: SpaceX agreed to indemnify the underwriters and their affiliates against certain liabilities and expenses, including Securities Act liabilities, in connection with sales of shares reserved for the DSP.
- **Beneficial ownership table exclusion**: The beneficial ownership table explicitly **does not reflect** any shares purchasable through the DSP.

## Online Brokerage Platforms and Retail Investor Allocations

Beyond allocations made to retail investors directly by the underwriters, SpaceX requested that certain shares be offered to retail investors through specific **selling group members** via their online brokerage platforms:

| Platform | Role / Affiliation |
|---|---|
| **Charles Schwab & Co., Inc.** | Selling group member |
| **Fidelity Brokerage Services LLC and Fidelity Capital Markets** (division of National Financial Services LLC) | Selling group member |
| **Robinhood Financial, LLC** | Selling group member |
| **SoFi Securities LLC** | Selling group member |
| **E*TRADE by Morgan Stanley** | Affiliate of Morgan Stanley & Co. LLC (underwriter) |

Key terms governing these retail allocations:

- **No affiliation with SpaceX**: The platforms are explicitly stated as **not affiliated** with the company.
- **Platform-specific terms**: Purchases are subject to the **terms, conditions, and requirements set by each selling group member**.
- **Price and timing parity**: Any purchase through these platforms will be at the **same IPO price** and at the **same time** as any other purchases in the offering, including those by institutions and other large investors.

## Restrictions on Discretionary Accounts

The underwriters informed SpaceX that **they do not intend sales to discretionary accounts to exceed 5%** of the total number of shares of Class A common stock offered by them. This is a standard IPO guardrail limiting the discretion underwriters have to allocate shares into accounts where the account holder has not specifically directed the purchase.

## Nasdaq Listing

SpaceX applied to list its Class A common stock on **Nasdaq** and **Nasdaq Texas** under the trading symbol **"SPCX."**

## Summary of Allocation Architecture

The offering's retail allocation framework thus operates through **three parallel channels**:

1. **Underwriter-directed retail allocations** — traditional institutional and retail distribution by the underwriting syndicate.
2. **Online brokerage platform sales** — shares offered at SpaceX's request through five named platforms (Schwab, Fidelity, Robinhood, SoFi, E*TRADE), all at the same price and time as institutional purchases.
3. **Directed Share Program** — a reserved percentage of shares for employees and designated individuals, administered by Morgan Stanley, with no lock-up, and with unsold balances reverting to the general public offering pool.

All three channels are constrained by the **5% discretionary account cap**, and the DSP and platform sales are explicitly designed to **reduce** the shares otherwise available to the general public only to the extent those reserved shares are actually purchased.

arguments.final_result:
## Principal Valuation, Underwriting, and Allocation Decisions That Cannot Be Finalized

The SpaceX S-1 (filed May 20, 2026) is explicitly preliminary: *"The information in this preliminary prospectus is not complete and may be changed."* The cover-page pricing table, share-count table, and numerous quantitative disclosures throughout the filing contain blank placeholders. Because the final offering size and price range are omitted, institutional investors cannot finalize the following categories of decisions.

---

### 1. VALUATION DECISIONS THAT CANNOT BE FINALIZED

**a. IPO Price and Implied Market Capitalization**
- The initial public offering price (per share and total) is **blank** on the cover page.
- The price range and its midpoint are referenced but **not filled in** (e.g., *"an initial public offering price of $[blank] per share … the midpoint of the price range set forth on the cover"*).
- Without a price, investors cannot compute the implied post-IPO equity market capitalization, enterprise value, or any per-share valuation multiple (P/S, P/B, EV/EBITDA, etc.) against the recast consolidated financials (which include xAI and X Holdings).

**b. Dilution to New Investors**
- Every figure in the dilution table is **blank**: pre-IPO net tangible book value per share, pro forma adjusted net tangible book value per share, the decrease attributable to new investors, and the dilution per share.
- The price-sensitivity analysis (impact of a $1.00 change in IPO price on dilution) is also **entirely blank**.
- The comparison table showing average price per share paid by Mr. Musk/existing investors versus new investors cannot be constructed.

**c. Pro Forma "As Adjusted" Capitalization**
- The "Pro Forma as Adjusted" column in the capitalization table (which gives effect to the offering) is **largely blank**, pending the final price and share count. Only the "Actual" and "Pro Forma" (pre-offering structural adjustments) columns are populated.

**d. Qualified IPO Threshold Confirmation**
- The Redeemable Convertible Preferred Stock (carrying value ~$7.0 billion for SpaceX preferred; ~$38 billion combined with xAI preferred) automatically converts into Class A and Class B common stock upon a "Qualified IPO," defined as:
  - Pre-offering market capitalization **≥ $6.0 billion**, AND
  - Aggregate cash proceeds to the company **≥ $250 million**.
- Because the offering price and size are blank, investors **cannot confirm whether these thresholds will be met**, and therefore cannot independently verify that the Preferred Conversion—a foundational pro forma assumption throughout the filing—will actually occur.

**e. Musk Performance Stock Award Milestones**
- The xAI-originated performance award grants Mr. Musk 0.20% of fully diluted capitalization upon each of 12 valuation milestones ranging from **$1.065 trillion to $6.565 trillion** (in $500 billion increments). The first milestone was achieved pre-merger. Whether subsequent milestones are triggered depends on the post-IPO valuation, which cannot be assessed without a price range.

**f. SEC Fee Table as a Floor Estimate**
- The SEC registration fee is stated as **$138,100**. At the FY2026 Section 6(b) rate of $138.10 per $1 million, this implies a proposed maximum aggregate offering price of approximately **$1.0 billion** for fee-calculation purposes only. This is a regulatory placeholder, not a confirmed offering size, and provides no usable price-per-share or final proceeds figure.

---

### 2. UNDERWRITING DECISIONS THAT CANNOT BE FINALIZED

**a. Underwriting Discounts and Commissions**
- Both the per-share and total underwriting discounts and commissions are **blank** on the cover page. Investors cannot assess the gross spread, the effective cost of capital, or the total compensation payable to the 23-firm syndicate (led by Goldman Sachs, Morgan Stanley, BofA, Citigroup, and J.P. Morgan as representatives).

**b. Net Proceeds to SpaceX**
- "Proceeds, before expenses, to SpaceX" is **blank** on the cover page.
- The Use of Proceeds section references net proceeds of **$[blank]** (or **$[blank]** if the over-allotment is exercised in full) at an assumed midpoint price of **$[blank]** per share. Investors cannot quantify the capital available for the stated uses (AI compute infrastructure, launch infrastructure, satellite constellations, and general corporate purposes).

**c. Over-Allotment (Greenshoe) Option Size**
- The number of additional shares purchasable under the 30-day over-allotment option is **blank**. Investors cannot assess the potential dilutive impact or the stabilizing effect of the greenshoe on post-IPO trading.

**d. Dealer Concession**
- The maximum per-share concession to selling dealers is **blank** (*"not in excess of $[blank] per share"*).

## Principal Valuation, Underwriting, and Allocation Decisions That Cannot Be Finalized

Because the final offering size and price range are omitted, institutional investors cannot finalize the following categories of decisions.

### 1. VALUATION DECISIONS THAT CANNOT BE FINALIZED

**a. IPO Price and Implied Market Capitalization**
- The initial public offering price (per share and total) is **blank** on the cover page.

- Without a price, investors cannot compute the implied post-IPO equity market capitalization, enterprise value, or any per-share valuation multiple (P/S, P/B, EV/EBITDA, etc.) against the recast consolidated financials (which include xAI and X Holdings).

**b. Dilution to New Investors**
- Every figure in the dilution table is **blank**: pre-IPO net tangible book value per share, pro forma adjusted net tangible book value per share, the decrease attributable to new investors, and the dilution per share.

- The comparison table showing average price per share paid by Mr. Musk/existing investors versus new investors cannot be constructed.

**c. Pro Forma "As Adjusted" Capitalization**
- The "Pro Forma as Adjusted" column in the capitalization table (which gives effect to the offering) is **largely blank**, pending the final price and share count. Only the "Actual" and "Pro Forma" (pre-offering structural adjustments) columns are populated.

**d. Qualified IPO Threshold Confirmation**
- The Redeemable Convertible Preferred Stock (carrying value ~$7.0 billion for SpaceX preferred; ~$38 billion combined with xAI preferred) automatically converts into Class A and Class B common stock upon a "Qualified IPO," defined as:
  - Pre-offering market capitalization **≥ $6.0 billion**, AND
  - Aggregate cash proceeds to the company **≥ $250 million**.
- Because the offering price and size are blank, investors **cannot confirm whether these thresholds will be met**, and therefore cannot independently verify that the Preferred Conversion—a foundational pro forma assumption throughout the filing—will actually occur.

- The xAI-originated performance award grants Mr. Musk 0.20% of fully diluted capitalization upon each of 12 valuation milestones ranging from **$1.065 trillion to $6.565 trillion** (in $500 billion increments).
